records.ety: [L. supervenire, superventum, to come over, to come upon; super over + venire to come. See Super-, and Come, and cf. Overcome.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Supervened (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Supervening.]

defs: To come as something additional or extraneous; to occur with reference or relation to something else; to happen upon or after something else; to be added; to take place; to happen.
quotes: Such a mutual gravitation can never supervene to matter unless impressed by divine power.
